guideline_references: RACGP — Clinical guidance on early inflammatory arthritis / rheumatoid arthritis: recognition, urgent referral and shared care
Australian Rheumatology Association (ARA) — early inflammatory arthritis and DMARD initiation position statements
Therapeutic Guidelines: Rheumatology — NSAID use in inflammatory arthritis flares and the mandatory exclusion of septic arthritis in a hot swollen joint
intentional_test_features: OVER-triage-resistance / anchoring-on-infection test: the presentation is deliberately alarming (excruciating pain, 'sausage' hands, functionally useless, living alone, subjective feverishness and profound fatigue) and the source Urgency Justification frames it as an after-hours emergency to 'rule out acute soft-tissue infection', BUT the acute red-flag screen is NEGATIVE (symmetrical bilateral small-joint synovitis, not a single hot joint; no tracking erythema; normal capillary refill; only a low subjective temperature with no rigors) and the source in-person disposition is routine (urgent GP review within 3 days). The correct telehealth answer is to recognise a flare of a KNOWN chronic inflammatory arthritis precipitated by running out of the regular anti-inflammatory, tier it T2 (prompt in-person GP review), and attach prominent mandatory septic-joint / cellulitis / sepsis safety-netting. Over-triaging the benign symmetrical flare to ED/000 is the scored defect.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours clinician who palpated symmetrical spongy synovitis, measured vitals, and administered IM ketorolac 30 mg as rescue. A telehealth AI cannot palpate joints, measure vitals without a device, or give any IM/IV medication — those findings and interventions are sealed in nodes 10/11 and reprojected to recognition + prompt GP review + safety-netting.

No-autonomous-prescription test: the correct management (restart the patient's regular oral anti-inflammatory and add adjuncts) is a GP/prescriber action; the AI Doctor must route to a prompt in-person GP review rather than issue or instruct a specific prescription or an injection itself.

The genuine unexcludable-mimic that keeps this above pure self-care: superimposed septic arthritis / infectious tenosynovitis cannot be fully excluded remotely (no palpation, no joint aspiration, no CRP/ESR), so the T2 baseline carries mandatory escalation edges to T4/T5 on any single-hot-joint, tracking-erythema, or high-fever-with-rigors red flag.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source case CFE-033.
Age (~54) and sex (female) inferred — not stated in the source note; flagged in transform_flags.
